name: Community Resource
description_short: Community Resource FCU is a member-owned credit union offering checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and credit cards to its members with a routing number 221373338.
description_long:
Community Resource Federal Credit Union (CRFCU) is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution serving consumers with a full spectrum of banking and lending products. Based on their website, they operate physical locations across multiple areas and provide NCUA-insured member accounts with dividend-bearing options. The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial services designed to serve their membership base.

Community Resource FCU provides personal checking accounts (including Free Checking, Dividend Checking, and Teen Checking for ages 13-17), multiple savings vehicles (Member Share Savings, Youth Savings up to age 17, SaveUp Account, Money Market Accounts), and certificate products (Share Certificates, Super Share Certificates, IRA Certificates). Their lending products include auto loans, boat/powersports/RV loans, personal loans, personal lines of credit, student loan refinancing, in-school student loans, and mortgage products (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA). They also issue multiple credit card options including rewards cards, cash back cards, secured cards, and business credit cards. Additional services include home equity loans, HELOCs, wealth management through LPL Financial, insurance products, and business banking services.

As a credit union, Community Resource FCU operates on a member-ownership model rather than shareholder profit maximization, which typically translates to more competitive rates and member-focused services. They provide digital banking through online and mobile platforms, message pay functionality, direct deposit, skip-a-pay options, and accessible customer service via phone (518.783.2211). Their learning resources include a financial blog, financial education partnerships with Balance Financial Fitness, and comprehensive FAQs across multiple banking functions.

The primary limitation is that membership is restricted to qualifying individuals within their defined service area or membership groups, making them inaccessible to the general public. Their website content is heavily focused on navigation and accessibility features rather than detailed product specifications, rates, or fee structures, which limits transparency for prospective members. No specific information is available regarding minimum balances, APRs, or competitive rate comparisons.
services: Free Checking and Dividend Checking accounts
Teen Checking (ages 13-17) and Youth Savings (up to age 17)
Member Share Savings and Money Market Accounts
Share Certificates, Super Share Certificates, and IRA Certificates
Auto loans, boat/powersports/RV loans, and personal loans
Mortgage products (conventional, FHA, VA, USDA) and home equity loans/HELOCs
Student loan refinancing and in-school student loans
Multiple credit cards (rewards, cash back, secured, business variants)
Wealth management through LPL Financial partnership
Business checking, savings, and merchant services
Online and mobile banking with message pay and skip-a-pay features
Debit cards with fraud controls and eAlerts
pros: Member-owned not-for-profit structure typically results in competitive rates and lower fees compared to traditional banks
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, certificates, mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, and business banking
Multiple credit card options with rewards programs (Everyday Rewards+, Max Cash Preferred, Travel Rewards+, etc.)
Youth-focused accounts including Teen Checking (ages 13-17) and Youth Savings (up to age 17)
Full mortgage suite with FHA, VA, and USDA loan options in addition to conventional mortgages
Digital banking convenience with online banking, mobile app, mobile check deposit, and message pay
Accessible customer service at 518.783.2211 with routing number 221373338 for direct transfers
cons: Membership restricted to specific service area or qualifying groups—not available to general public
Website lacks specific rate information, APRs, fees, or product comparison details needed for informed decision-making
No transparent information about minimum account balances or deposit requirements
Limited details on eligibility requirements for membership and specific underwriting standards
Website structure and navigation-heavy design provides minimal substantive product details
best_for: Individuals within CRFCU's service area seeking full-service banking with competitive member rates
Families wanting youth banking products for teenagers and young savers
Small business owners needing business checking, savings, and commercial credit cards
Homebuyers looking for mortgage options including FHA and VA loan programs
diagnosis: Community Resource FCU is best for individuals within their service area who qualify for membership and seek a full-service credit union with competitive rates, member-focused service, and diverse product offerings across personal and business banking. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted and not universally available, requiring qualification within their defined service population or geographic area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
